texto: But having people meet my family was a secret fear. It would be like taking someone to a dark room to show them my anal fissures, and you can't just go introducing everybody to your anal fissures. Only special people get to see such as that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Harrison Scott Key: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “But having people meet my family was a secret fear. It would be like taking someone to a dark roo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, anal, family pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e family.

texto: In one terrible instant, that terrible thing happened, the single most tragic experience of my, and just about any, childhood: boredom.

frase_original: In one terrible instant, that terrible thing happened, the single most tragic experience of my, and just about any, childhood: boredom.

texto: Was I the only one who became unsettled and swoonish at the sight of a large, inverted carcass hanging from a tree, its vital organs strewn about like children's toys, the occasional pack of hunting dogs fighting over a lung, another one looking for a quiet place to enjoy the severed head? It happened all the time and nobody else seemed bothered. People just walked up to the bloody carcasses and carried on entirely normal conversations, as though a man wasn't standing there squeezing deer feces out of a large intestine and small children weren't playing football with a liver.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Harrison Scott Key: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Was I the only one who became unsettled and swoonish at the sight of a large, inverted carcass ha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, deer, food-for-thought pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de food-for-thought e humor.
